title: Hierarchical Delegation
constraints: A manager agent must decompose the task into subtasks
Each subtask must be delegated to an appropriate specialist agent
The manager must synthesize specialist outputs into a final answer
Specialists must not communicate directly with each other
hints: In CrewAI, use Process.hierarchical with a manager_llm to enable automatic delegation
In LangGraph, create a manager node that routes to specialist nodes and collects their outputs
The manager should break a complex request into distinct sub-questions before delegating

starterCode.crewai:
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew, Process
from crewai import LLM

llm = LLM(model="gpt-4o-mini")

# BUG: A single agent tries to do everything — no delegation
# TODO: Add specialist agents and a manager that delegates subtasks
generalist = Agent(
    role="Business Consultant",
    goal="Analyze a business idea covering market, finance, and technical feasibility",
    backstory="You are a generalist consultant.",
    llm=llm,
)

analysis_task = Task(
    description="Provide a full business analysis for: {idea}",
    expected_output="A comprehensive report covering market analysis, financial projections, and technical feasibility",
    agent=generalist,
)

crew = Crew(
    agents=[generalist],
    tasks=[analysis_task],
    process=Process.sequential,
)

result = crew.kickoff(inputs={"idea": "AI-powered personal finance app"})
print(result)
starterCode.langgraph:
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from langgraph.graph import StateGraph, START, END
from typing import TypedDict

llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o-mini")

class State(TypedDict):
    idea: str
    result: str

# BUG: A single node does all the analysis — no decomposition or delegation
# TODO: Add a manager that decomposes and delegates to specialist nodes
def analyze(state: State) -> dict:
    prompt = f"Provide a full business analysis for: {state['idea']}. Cover market analysis, financial projections, and technical feasibility."
    response = llm.invoke(prompt)
    return {"result": response.content}

graph = StateGraph(State)
graph.add_node("analyze", analyze)
graph.add_edge(START, "analyze")
graph.add_edge("analyze", END)

app = graph.compile()
result = app.invoke({"idea": "AI-powered personal finance app", "result": ""})
print(result["result"])
starterCode.openai-agents-sdk:
from agents import Agent, Runner

# BUG: A single agent tries to do everything — no delegation
# TODO: Add specialist agents and a manager that delegates subtasks
agent = Agent(
    name="Business Consultant",
    instructions="You are a generalist consultant. Analyze a business idea covering market, finance, and technical feasibility.",
)

result = Runner.run_sync(agent, "Provide a full business analysis for: AI-powered personal finance app. Cover market analysis, financial projections, and technical feasibility.")
print(result.final_output)

evaluationCriteria.label: Manager decomposes the task
evaluationCriteria.description: The manager agent breaks the complex task into distinct subtasks

evaluationCriteria.label: Specialist agents exist
evaluationCriteria.description: There are at least two specialist agents with distinct domains (e.g., market, finance, technical)

evaluationCriteria.label: Delegation to specialists works
evaluationCriteria.description: Each subtask is handled by the appropriate specialist, not the manager

evaluationCriteria.label: Manager synthesizes final output
evaluationCriteria.description: The manager combines specialist outputs into a coherent final report
